summary: Reuters reported Coinbase will cut about 700 jobs, or about 14% of its global workforce, in a restructuring aimed at reducing costs and repositioning the crypto exchange for the artificial intelligence era.
evidenceQuote: Coinbase said on Tuesday it will cut about 700 jobs, or about 14% of its global workforce, as part of a restructuring plan aimed at reducing costs and repositioning the business for the artificial intelligence era.
notes: Reuters also reported CEO Brian Armstrong cited rapid advances in AI, saying new tools allow non-technical teams to ship code and automate tasks that previously required larger headcounts. Market volatility and cost reduction are also cited drivers, so the entry is explicit-AI-cited but not framed as AI as the only cause.

sources.name: Reuters
sources.quote: CEO Brian Armstrong also cited rapid advances in artificial intelligence, saying new tools were allowing non-technical teams to ship code and automate tasks that previously required larger headcounts.
